data.task:
Ticket 0046 — ATOM 1 of 3 (taxonomy/format owner layer). Make the wrap-up Play's Run Status vocabulary TARGET-AWARE, and pin it. Do NOT touch runner.ts or launcher.ts in this atom (those are atoms 2 and 3).

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1) `packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md` — the fenced founder-closeout contract. The `**Run Status**` section is currently priority-shaped only (`continue | blocked | archive ready`). Make it explicitly target-aware:
   - PRIORITY-launched runs keep: `continue | blocked | archive ready` (unchanged meaning; `archive ready` asks for founder archive confirmation, never self-archive).
   - TICKET-launched runs use: `needs another run | closed | needs closing | blocked`.
   - Document the CLOSE-OR-ASK semantics in plain prose under Run Status (and reinforce in the Founder Decision Needed section if needed):
     * `closed` is ONLY for a verified-complete ticket fix that is closed through the `closeTicket()` spine at the wrap boundary (prunes order.json, stamps a `## Resolution`). It is the ticket analogue of a completed run.
     * `needs closing` REQUIRES a non-None Founder Decision Needed carrying the explicit close decision — use it when completion is not proven or founder judgment is needed before closing.
     * Never leave a verified-fixed ticket implicitly waiting for teardown — a ticket run must close (`closed`) or ask (`needs closing`).
     * Do NOT map a ticket `closed` onto the priority `archive ready` confirmation flow; they are distinct.

HARD CONSTRAINTS (single owner per concept — ticket note 'do not copy the vocabulary into a second local contract'):
   - Keep ALL existing bold section headings byte-identical (`**Atom Complete**`, `**Run Status**`, `**What Changed**`, `**Judgment:**`, `**What Remains**`, `**Founder Decision Needed**`, `**Commit State**`, `**Recommended Next Step**`, `**Teardown Readiness**`) and the final `I'm standing by...` line. The runner parses the closeout contract by these exact section labels; do NOT rename, reorder, or remove any. Only enrich the CONTENT of the Run Status (and, if helpful, Founder Decision Needed) section. This keeps the runner's contract parser intact.
   - Do NOT add the vocabulary as a machine-readable list anywhere else; the Play is the single format owner.

2) PIN: `packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts` — add/extend assertions so the test proves BOTH vocabularies are present in the wrap-up Play (the priority set `continue | blocked | archive ready` AND the ticket set `needs another run | closed | needs closing | blocked`), and that the contract is NOT duplicated (single fenced founder-closeout block / single Run Status owner). Match the existing assertion style in that file; do not weaken any existing assertion into a tautology.

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT CRITERIA (run these yourself and paste the real exit codes + output):
   - `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -F @cocoder/personas test` (or the repo's equivalent for the personas package) is GREEN, including your new assertions. If the package name differs, discover it from the workspace and use the correct one.
   - `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -F @cocoder/core test` is GREEN — this proves the runner's founder-closeout contract PARSER still parses the edited Play (regression guard for the heading-preservation constraint). If core tests reference the Play contract, they must still pass unchanged.
   - Typecheck clean for any package you touched.

MUST NOT BREAK: runner enforcement behavior (you are not editing it); the closeout contract parser; any existing wrap-up Play section. Report the exact files changed and the full test command output.

data.task:
Ticket 0046 — ATOM 2 of 3 (runner enforcement layer). Make the founder-closeout VALIDATION + wrap DISPOSITION derivation in `packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts` target-aware (priority vs ticket), and expose an explicit ticket-close decision on `RunResult` for the launcher (atom 3) to gate on. Do NOT touch `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` close-gate logic in this atom — only add the signal it will consume. The wrap-up Play already landed in atom 0 with target-aware vocabulary; this atom enforces it.

CONTEXT YOU MUST READ FIRST: runner.ts lines ~224-367 (FounderCloseoutContract, parseFounderCloseoutContract, founderCloseoutSection, founderDecisionNeeded, deriveWrapupRunStatus, deriveWrapDisposition), ~415-504 (founderCloseoutFormatIssues, validatePlayOutput, PlayOutputValidationInput), ~506-520 (formatInvalidFounderCloseoutFallback), the wrap site ~1652-1688 (where validatePlayOutput / deriveWrapDisposition / deriveWrapupRunStatus are called and `input.ticketId` is in scope), the RunResult interface ~180-197, and the terminal return ~1787-1839. The launch target is known via `input.ticketId` (truthy => ticket-launched run). The Play's Run Status section now contains two parseable lines: `Priority-launched run: continue | blocked | archive ready.` and `Ticket-launched run: needs another run | closed | needs closing | blocked.`

WHAT TO CHANGE (runner.ts):
1) SINGLE OWNER — DERIVE the vocab, do NOT hard-code it. The wrap-up Play is the format owner (shared-standards: runtime surfaces must parse/derive from the owner, never copy its allowed values into a second local contract). Extend the closeout-contract parsing so the runner reads the allowed Run Status values for each target from the Play's Run Status section (parse the `Priority-launched run: …` and `Ticket-launched run: …` `A | B | C` lists). Use those derived sets for enforcement below. Do NOT introduce a second hard-coded copy of the vocabulary in runner.ts.
2) Thread the launch target through the wrap site: compute `isTicket = Boolean(input.ticketId)` and pass it into validation + both derivations.
3) `founderCloseoutFormatIssues` (and its validatePlayOutput entry) become target-aware: a TICKET closeout's Run Status must be one of the ticket set and MUST be rejected if it is `archive ready` (priority-only); a ticket Run Status of `needs closing` MUST require a non-None Founder Decision Needed (reject `needs closing` + `None`). A PRIORITY closeout keeps its set and must reject ticket-only values (`closed`, `needs closing`, `needs another run`). Keep all existing format checks intact.
4) `deriveWrapupRunStatus` target-aware terminal status: ticket `closed` => `completed` (so the existing launcher close gate can fire); ticket `needs closing` => `awaiting-founder`; priority `archive ready` => `awaiting-founder` (unchanged); otherwise existing behavior (founderDecisionNeeded => awaiting-founder else completed). Ticket `needs another run` => `completed` (it is the `continue` analogue — the run finished its atoms cleanly).
5) NEW SIGNAL for atom 3: add a field to `RunResult` — `ticketCloseDecision: 'close' | 'ask' | 'none'`. Derive it in the wrap path from the ticket Run Status: `closed` => 'close'; `needs closing` => 'ask'; everything else (including ALL priority runs and ticket `needs another run`/`blocked`) => 'none'. Make the field REQUIRED on the interface and set it explicitly at EVERY `RunResult` return site (stopRun, holdRun, the early-exit returns, and the terminal return) — default `'none'` for every non-ticket-wrap path. The compiler must force all sites; fix any daemon/test RunResult constructors that break by adding `ticketCloseDecision: 'none'`. (Atom 3's launcher will close the ticket ONLY when this === 'close' — this is what prevents a `needs another run` wrap, which also resolves `completed`, from wrongly closing the ticket.)
6) `formatInvalidFounderCloseoutFallback` and `deriveWrapDisposition`: make the fallback text and disposition derivation target-aware so a ticket fallback does not emit priority `archive ready` vocabulary, and ticket `closed` is NOT mapped onto the priority `archive-candidate` disposition.

PIN: `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts` — add tests proving, on PRIMARY behavior (call the exported functions / validatePlayOutput directly with priority-target and ticket-target inputs):
   - the contract parser extracts BOTH vocab sets from the Play (priority set and ticket set).
   - ticket validator REJECTS `archive ready`; ACCEPTS `closed` and `needs closing` (with required Founder Decision Needed); REJECTS `needs closing` + `None`.
   - priority validator REJECTS ticket-only values (`closed`).
   - `deriveWrapupRunStatus`: ticket `closed` => `completed`; ticket `needs closing` => `awaiting-founder`; ticket `needs another run` => `completed`.
   - `ticketCloseDecision`: ticket `closed` => 'close'; ticket `needs closing` => 'ask'; ticket `needs another run` => 'none'; any priority => 'none'.
   Match the existing test style/helpers in runner.test.ts; do not weaken existing assertions.

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT CRITERIA (run yourself; paste real exit codes + output):
   - `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -F @cocoder/core test` GREEN (incl. new tests AND the existing 509 still passing — runner contract parser regression).
   - `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -F @cocoder/daemon test` GREEN (no launcher behavior change; only RunResult constructor sites updated to add `ticketCloseDecision: 'none'`). If the daemon suite has unrelated pre-existing failures, report them but do not fix out of scope.
   - Typecheck clean across touched packages (`@cocoder/core`, and `@cocoder/daemon` if you touched a constructor there).

MUST NOT BREAK: launcher close-gate behavior (untouched this atom); the wrap-up Play (do not edit it); existing format checks; the existing 509 core tests. Report exact files changed and full test output.

data.task:
Ticket 0046 — ATOM 3 of 3 (launcher close-timing layer). Make the ticket close gate in `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` gate on the new `RunResult.ticketCloseDecision` signal (added in atom 1) instead of the blunt `result.status === 'completed'`, and pin the close-or-ask behavior. This is the layer that prevents the strand at the source.

CONTEXT YOU MUST READ FIRST: `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` `closeTicketAfterSuccessfulRun` (lines ~424-475) and its call site (~689). The `RunResult` now carries `ticketCloseDecision: 'close' | 'ask' | 'none'` (exported from @cocoder/core). atom 1 (runner) already maps: ticket `closed` wrap => terminalStatus `completed` AND `ticketCloseDecision: 'close'`; ticket `needs closing` => `awaiting-founder` + `'ask'`; ticket `needs another run`/`blocked` => `'none'`; all priority runs => `'none'`. `closeTicket()` already prunes `order.json` and stamps `## Resolution` (see `packages/core/src/tickets/close.ts`).

WHY THIS IS NEEDED (the actual bug): the current gate closes a ticket whenever the run resolved `completed`. A ticket wrap of `needs another run` ALSO resolves `completed` (it is the `continue` analogue), so today it would WRONGLY close a ticket whose fix is not done. Gating on `ticketCloseDecision === 'close'` closes ONLY a verified-complete `closed` wrap.

WHAT TO CHANGE (launcher.ts):
1) In `closeTicketAfterSuccessfulRun`, replace the `if (result.status !== 'completed') return` gate (and the redundant run-row completed check, your judgment whether to keep it as defense-in-depth) with a gate on `result.ticketCloseDecision`:
   - `ticketCloseDecision === 'close'` => proceed with the EXISTING closeTicket()/commitGovernance()/reconcile/audit logic UNCHANGED (close, prune order.json, stamp Resolution, commit through the spine, audit `ticket-close`). Keep the existing `!close.closed` stale-order reconcile path intact inside this branch.
   - `ticketCloseDecision === 'ask'` => do NOT close; leave the ticket open. The founder close decision is already surfaced (run is `awaiting-founder` with a non-None Founder Decision Needed in the closeout). Append an audit trace (e.g. `action: 'ticket-close-deferred'`, ticketId, runId, reason: wrap requested founder close decision) so the deferral is visible. Return.
   - `ticketCloseDecision === 'none'` => do NOT close; return (no audit needed, or a light skip audit if it matches existing style). This is what stops a `needs another run`/`blocked` ticket wrap from closing an unfinished ticket.
   Do not otherwise change the close/reconcile/commit machinery or the call site at ~689.

PIN: `packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts` — keep the existing `closed`-path test passing (it already asserts the ticket closes, INDEX moves it to Recently Closed, and `order.json` is pruned to the remaining ids). ADD two scenarios using the ticket closeout helper (`validTicketFounderCloseout` in `tests/helpers/founder-closeout.ts`):
   a) UNCERTAIN wrap: `runHeadless` returns `validTicketFounderCloseout('needs closing', 'Yes — <explicit founder close decision text>')`. Assert: the ticket stays OPEN (not closed, still in `## Open`), `order.json` STILL contains the id (it was NOT pruned), the run resolves `awaiting-founder`, and the founder close decision is surfaced (the run is awaiting-founder / the closeout carries a non-None Founder Decision Needed). Assert NO `ticket-close` commit happened.
   b) NEEDS-ANOTHER-RUN regression guard: `runHeadless` returns `validTicketFounderCloseout('needs another run')`. Assert: the run resolves `completed` BUT the ticket is NOT closed and `order.json` is UNCHANGED (still contains the id). This proves a completed-but-not-`closed` ticket wrap no longer strands-closes the ticket.
   Match the existing test setup/patterns in this file (the order.json seeding at ~804, the readTickets/INDEX/order.json assertions at ~844-865). Do not weaken any existing assertion. If you need a status read, use the same store/run-record surface the existing tests use.

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT CRITERIA (run yourself; paste real exit codes + output):
   - `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -F @cocoder/daemon test` GREEN, including the two new scenarios AND all existing mutations tests.
   - `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' -F @cocoder/core test` GREEN (no core change expected; regression check).
   - Typecheck clean for `@cocoder/daemon`.

MUST NOT BREAK: the existing `closed`-path close + order.json prune + reconcile tests; the close/commit spine; the call site. Do NOT touch runner.ts, the wrap-up Play, or core. Report exact files changed and full test output.
